Off Calendar Apr-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy D.F. Barteau of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for intervenors / moving party Intervenors as listed below
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Andre Wright

RULING:
INTERVENORS ST. PAUL FIRE AND MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY, WAUSAU INSURANCE COMPANY, ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANIES, NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG, PA, AND CERTAIN SOLVENT LONDON MARKET INSURANCE COMPANIES ON BEHALF OF THEIR SUSPENDED INSURED, DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Company, Wausau Insurance Company, Argonaut Insurance Companies, National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg, PA, and certain Solvent London Market Insurance Companies on behalf of their suspended insured Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 3 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability), granted as to issue 2 (strict liability) and off calendar as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and adjudication issues 1 and 3, intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Wright was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to Scott Co. Intervenors' objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled, except for intervenors' objections to plaintiff's identification of Garlock-brand gaskets, which are sustained because plaintiff testified during his deposition that he did not know the brand or manufacturer of the gaskets. As to adjudication issue 2, intervenors sustained their initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether intervenors are liable under a strict liability theory. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
